1:
Both FIFO and SJF response time and turnaround time will be the same if the order is preserved. Response time job 0: 0, job 1: 100, job 2: 300 Turnaround time job 0: 100, job 1: 300, job 2 600 Average response time: 200 Average turnaround time: 333.33 But if the job length are not in ascending order: For example, -1 300, 200, 100: 1 
 FIFO: Response time job 0: 0, job 1: 300, job 2: 500 Turnaround time job 0: 300, job 1: 500, job 2 600 Average response time: 266.67 Average turnaround time: 466.67. The average response time and average turnaround time will increase if the order is changed for FIFO.
3: The main problem is only one thread could get the mutex of vectors, even if other threads are requiring different vectors. Vector-avoid-hold-and-wait has a shorter total time taken when running with -p, while vector-global-order and vector-try-wait have even shorter total time taken when running with -p than without.
4: It takes 9 time units to complete
5: When the buffer is increased, the behavior of the code do not change a lot. Producer produces one then consumer consumes one. When the buffer size was set to 10, num_full is still change back and forth between 0 and 1.
